texto: People want to think of economics as a natural science, like physics, with the comforting reliability of simple-to-understand theories like F=MA. Unfortunately, it isn't. Economics is a social science, and the so-called theories are really social and moral constructs.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Nick Hanauer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “People want to think of economics as a natural science, like physics, with the comforting reliabi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, economics, people pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de people e moral.

texto: Economics is mostly how humans rationalize who gets what and why. It's how we instantiate our preferences about status, privileges, and power.
explicacao: Citação de Nick Hanauer sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Economics is mostly how humans rationalize who gets what and why. It's how we instantiate our pre…” articula reflexao, economics, why em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como why e status.

frase_original: Economics is mostly how humans rationalize who gets what and why. It's how we instantiate our preferences about status, privileges, and power.

texto: The person earning the federal minimum wage of $7.25 isn't going out to eat at restaurants. They're not taking piano lessons. They're not going to the gym or the yoga studio. They're not sending mom flowers on Mother's day. What good is this person in the economy? If you raise it to $15 an hour, they're doing all of those things.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Nick Hanauer que provoca temas ligados a familia. Ao questionar “The person earning the federal minimum wage of $7.25 isn't going out to eat at restaurants. They'…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, mother, mom, com foco em termino e academia.
